title: triticum vagans
side_effects: Triticum vagans can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues. Ingesting large quantities of the plant's extract can lead to stomach pain, nausea, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oils may cause headaches, dizziness, and respiratory problems. Improper handling and preparation of the plant can also result in eye irritation and skin rashes.
